titleStmt.title: Uppsala, Uppsala University Library, O Etiop. 11
Miracles of Mary

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation in pencil in the upper outer corner.

desc.title: ʾƎsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki wa-ʾǝweddǝsaki

item.desc: Note recording the purchase of the book by Gabra ʾIyasus, his donation of the book to Tābota Ṣǝyon, and an exhortation for the priests and deacons who read it to pray for him and his wife. It is unclear if the Tābota Ṣǝyon that is mentioned is a church or perhaps only a tābot.
item.q: ዛቲ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ዘተሣየጠ፡ በንዋዩ፡ ገብረ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ወወሀባ፡ ለተቦተጽዮን፡ ከመ፡ ታንብሮ፡ ደርገ፡ ምስለ፡ ምእመናን፡ አመ፡ ዕለተ፡ ፍዳ፡ ወደይን፡ ወምስለ፡ ቡሩካን፡ ጻድቃን፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፡ እንዘ፡ ያነ፡ ብቡ፡ ቀሳውስት፡ ወዲያቆናት፡ ለእመ፡ ኢተ፡ ዘክሩ፡ ስሞ፡ ወስመ፡ ብእሲቱ፡ ወለተ፡ ኪዳን፡ ውጉዛነ፡ ይኩኑ፡ በስልጣነ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ወጳውሎስ፡ ወበ፡ አፈ፡ ቄርሎስ፡

item.desc: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, Jesus separated the Virgin's soul from her body or, according to The Princeton Ethiopian, Eritrean, and Egyptian Miracles of MaryWendy LauraBelcherJeremy R.Brown2018https://pemm.princeton.edu/ (see , Miracle of Mary: Five Pains of the Virgin, Conversation of the Virgin and Jesus Christ; promise of salvation to those who recite the Paternoster and Ave Maria.
Note recording the purchase of the book by Gabra ʾIyasus containing an exhortation for the priests and deacons who read it to pray for him and his wife.
item.q: ዛቲ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ዘተሣየጠ፡ በንዋዩ፡ ገብረ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ኦዘያነብቡ፡ ካህናት፡ ወዲያቆናት፡ ለእመ፡ ኢጸውኡ፡ ስሞ፡ ወስመ፡ ብእሲቱ፡ ወለተ፡ ኪዳን፡ ውጉዛነ፡ ይኩኑ፡ በሥልጣነ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ወጳውሎስ፡

item.desc: Paper sticker on the inside of the front board.
Note in Swedish recording the donation of the manuscript by August Bergman to its current repository in 1894.
item.q:
Etiopisk handskrift n. 4
skänkt d. 3 Sept. 1894 till Upsala
Universitetsbibliotek af August Bergman
svenskt missionär i Belleza, Asmara,
i Abyssinien

history.provenance: originally belonged to Śəlṭāna Krəstos (cf. ), whereas —possibly produced to complement —originally belonged to Walda Giyorgis. Secondary owner of at least was Gabra ʾIyasus.
history.acquisition: According to , the manuscript reached its current repository in . It was a gift from August Bergman, who had been active as a missionary in Balazā.

bibl.title: Katalog über die äthiopischen Handschriften in der Universitätsbibliothek Uppsala, Sowie Anhänge über äthiopische Handschriften in anderen Bibliotheken und in Privatbesitz in Schweden
Acta Bibliothecae R. Universitatis Upsaliensis
Die Abessinischen Handschriften der Königl. Universitätsbibliothek zu Upsala
Zeitschrift der Deutschen Morgenländischen Gesellschaft
Beta maṣāḥǝft: Manuscripts of Ethiopia and Eritrea
Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>
Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della Vida
Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente
The Princeton Ethiopian, Eritrean, and Egyptian Miracles of Mary

msItem.title: Tafaśśǝḥi ʾo-za-nǝsǝʾǝlaki dāḫnā

handNote.desc: According to Katalog über die äthiopischen Handschriften in der Universitätsbibliothek Uppsala, Sowie Anhänge über äthiopische Handschriften in anderen Bibliotheken und in Privatbesitz in SchwedenActa Bibliothecae R. Universitatis UpsaliensisOscarLöfgrenUppsalaAlmqvist & Wiksell1974Acta Bibliothecae R. Universitatis Upsaliensis181895, the hand is "young and angular" (jung, eckig); Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952151 describes it as a "crude hand" (rozza mano).

origin.origDate: 1850–1900. Walda Giyorgis, introduced as ʾabuya (an attempt to transfer the title ʾabuna to a first person singular context?) and his son Gərmā Ṣəyon are mentioned in supplications. It is possible that this manuscript part was produced to complement .

msItem.title: Taʾammǝra Māryām
Maṣḥafa śǝrʿāt za-waṣʾa ʾǝm-manbara Mārqos ḥawāryā ʾǝm-makāna maʿāllǝqā
Introductive exhortation to the Taʾammǝra Māryām
Seventy-two Miracles of Mary
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo
Miracle of Mary: Homily on a potter from Syria
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
Miracle of Mary: St John sees the Virgin in glory
msItem.note: identifies this miracle as Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, meeting with St John.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a monk in the monastery of St. Ephrem, on an island in the river of Tigris
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a fountain of water caused by Jesus in Māḫḍāb while returning to Palestine
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Convent of Māḫǝw
Miracle of Mary: St Mary comes to a church in the form of a white dove to heal from smallpox a man whose only good work was celebrating her commemoration
msItem.note: The Princeton Ethiopian, Eritrean, and Egyptian Miracles of MaryWendy LauraBelcherJeremy R.Brown2018https://pemm.princeton.edu/ (at ) identifies this miracle as Miracle of Mary: St Mary comes to a church in the form of a white dove to heal from smallpox a man whose only good work was celebrating her commemoration, and also as Miracle of Mary: St Mary appears to the disciples of Mār Barsumā in the form of a dove.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jewish boy from Tyrus, saved by Mary from the furnace
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the drunk monk Timotewos (?), saved by the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary and her Son appeared in a beehive
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Anastasius, a deacon of Rome
Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary rebuked a woman who gave up fasting at her husband's command
Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary delivered from the devils the soul of an avaricious farmer who had given alms in her name
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew of ʾAḫmīm
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears as Miracle 69.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears as Miracle 70.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Bishop of Rome, ʾAbbās (Leo)
Miracle of Mary: Yǝsḥaq, prior of Dabra Mǝṭmāq, who fled to Scete to escape the burden of his office
msItem.note: The Princeton Ethiopian, Eritrean, and Egyptian Miracles of MaryWendy LauraBelcherJeremy R.Brown2018https://pemm.princeton.edu/ (at ) identifies this miracle as Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the monk Isaac.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the young girl called Mary, from Dafrā
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a French painter whose scaffold was overthrown by the devil
Miracle of a sick man in a hospital whom Mary carries to Jerusalem
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the youth Zechariah, from Rome
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Juliana and Barbara from Bethlehem
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears as Miracle 68.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of three Arabs in Rīf and Salomon, abbot of Kalamon
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the construction of a church in Jericho
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the blind priest John from the church of St Mercurius in Cairo
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a rich man from Qʷalāsis who fought the Qʷǝz and whose eye was pierced by an arrow
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Abraham, his wife Gerā and their blind daughter Elisabeth from Badramān
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a widow and her poor daughters Maria, Martha, and Yawāhit
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the two scribes who were brothers
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of George the Younger, who was delivered from prison
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the old priest Qaṭīr at ʾƎlkǝsus (al-Khusus)
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the two brothers Tāg and Nazīb from Dalgā
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a European man with a petrified foot
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the leprous Bishop Mercurius
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a woman from Hartalom/Hartarom with broken legs
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Phoenician evil man called Bārok
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Anastasius, a deacon of Rome
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952168 observes that this miracles appears three times in the manuscript: as Miracle 12, as Miracle 39, and as Miracle 61. Miracle 12 and Miracle 61 represent the same recension, whereas Miracle 39 represents another.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a monk of the Monastery of St Samuel of Kalamon
Miracle of Mary: The Story of the Cannibal of Qǝmr
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor widow and her son who became a thief and was crucified
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a pregnant woman
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears—in another recension—as Miracle 67.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of an evil officer at Tarsus in Cilicia who loved Mary and had an apparition of Jesus
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a rich man who cast a loaf to a beggar and was saved by the Virgin after being condemned by Jesus
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Qirās, a brigand of Galatia, saved by Mary from hanging
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a woman who prayed for a child and sees three sons in an apparition
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a one-eyed woman healed by Mary in Bethlehem
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the water reservoir at the Convent of Mǝṭmāq
Miracle of Mary: Story of an apparition of Jesus at the Convent of Qʷǝsqʷām to a monk called Anton (Antoine)
msItem.note: The Princeton Ethiopian, Eritrean, and Egyptian Miracles of MaryWendy LauraBelcherJeremy R.Brown2018https://pemm.princeton.edu/ (at ) identifies this miracle as Miracle of Mary: The blessing given to the animals at Dabra Qʷǝsqʷām by the anointing of St Mary.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: When Sǝfǝngǝyā, wife of king Masfǝyānos of Romǝyā, prays to have a child before the icon of St Mary, the icon inclines its head; Sǝfǝngǝyā then conceives Yǝsḥaq (ʾAbbā Garimā)
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, Jews burned in Bethlehem
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of an image of Christ, painted by St John for Tiberius, revelations by Mary to St John
Miracle of Mary: Homily on the founding of the Convent of Mǝṭmāq
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Yoḥannǝs Kǝmā who founded a Convent
msItem.note: The Princeton Ethiopian, Eritrean, and Egyptian Miracles of MaryWendy LauraBelcherJeremy R.Brown2018https://pemm.princeton.edu/ (at ) identifies this miracle as Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the monk Yoḥannǝs (?) of St Macarius who had visions of the Virgin.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of sunken ship saved by Mary
Miracle of Mary: How a great king in Rome dedicated an altar to Virgin Mary and Her Child
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the sinful deacon ʾAstirās whom Mary ordered to be buried in the believers' graveyard
Miracle of Mary: How Nicodemus the Persian knight became a Christian
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Bishop Philoteus
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Anastasius, a deacon of Rome
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952168 observes that this miracles appears three times in the manuscript: as Miracle 12, as Miracle 39, and as Miracle 61. Miracle 12 and Miracle 61 represent the same recension, whereas Miracle 39 represents another.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the monk Gerdan obeyed the lust of the flesh and cut his own throat and how the Virgin Mary brought his soul back to his body
Miracle of Mary: How a bishop removed a priest from his office and how the Virgin Mary rebuked the bishop
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a shipwrecked pilgrim
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a sinful woman who swallowed a scorpion
Miracle of Mary: An abbot in Scete sees first a multitude of demons, then a multitude of saints
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a pregnant woman
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears—in another recension—as Miracle 43.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Juliana and Barbara from Bethlehem
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears as Miracle 23.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew of ʾAḫmīm
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears as Miracle 15.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi
msItem.note: Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 observes that this miracles also appears as Miracle 16.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the image of the Virgin Mary in a Church bowed to the monks

handNote.desc: According to Katalog über die äthiopischen Handschriften in der Universitätsbibliothek Uppsala, Sowie Anhänge über äthiopische Handschriften in anderen Bibliotheken und in Privatbesitz in SchwedenActa Bibliothecae R. Universitatis UpsaliensisOscarLöfgrenUppsalaAlmqvist & Wiksell1974Acta Bibliothecae R. Universitatis Upsaliensis181895, the hand is "good" (gut) and dates from circa 1800. Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952151 describes it as a "better hand" (una migliore mano) and dates the hand to the 19th century.

Äthiopische PaläographieÄthiopistische ForschungenSiegbertUhligStuttgartFranz Steiner Verlag1988Äthiopistische Forschungen2222527–528 describes the hand in detail and dates it to the first half of the 17th century. According to Katalog über die äthiopischen Handschriften in der Universitätsbibliothek Uppsala, Sowie Anhänge über äthiopische Handschriften in anderen Bibliotheken und in Privatbesitz in SchwedenActa Bibliothecae R. Universitatis UpsaliensisOscarLöfgrenUppsalaAlmqvist & Wiksell1974Acta Bibliothecae R. Universitatis Upsaliensis181895, the hand is "strong and regular" (kräftig, regelmäßig) and dates from c. 1600. Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952151 describes it as a "beautiful writing" (bella scrittura) and dates the hand to the beginning of the 17th century or the end of the 16th century.

According to Katalog über die äthiopischen Handschriften in der Universitätsbibliothek Uppsala, Sowie Anhänge über äthiopische Handschriften in anderen Bibliotheken und in Privatbesitz in SchwedenActa Bibliothecae R. Universitatis UpsaliensisOscarLöfgrenUppsalaAlmqvist & Wiksell1974Acta Bibliothecae R. Universitatis Upsaliensis181895, the hand is similar to but smaller (ähnlich aber kleiner) and dates from the 18th century. Due codici di Upsala del <hi rend="italics">Libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria</hi>Studi orientalistici in onore di Giorgio Levi Della VidaPubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’OrienteEnricoCerulliRomaIstituto per l’Oriente1956Pubblicazioni dell’Istituto per l’Oriente52I151–17952170 just notes that the hand is different from .

history.origin: 1600–1650 The original owner was Śəlṭāna Krəstos. A later owner, as emerged both from and numerous additions in supplications, was Gabra ʾIyasus. It is unclear if formed part of the manuscript when Gabra ʾIyasus owned it.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)
